name: Filter: Posterize Bitmap
description: Trace any photo into flat, screenprint-style vector colour separations.

inputs.label: Photo
inputs.help: The image to posterize. Headshots and high-contrast photos trace best.

inputs.label: Colour steps
inputs.help: How many tonal separations to trace. Changing this re-samples the colours from the photo.

inputs.label: Threshold (1 ink)
inputs.help: Collapse the photo to a single ink over paper — one hard cut between dark and light instead of graded separations. Pair with Transparent background for ink on nothing.

inputs.label: Threshold level
inputs.help: Where the cut between ink and paper falls. Higher turns more of the photo into ink; lower keeps only the deepest shadows.

inputs.label: Brightness
inputs.help: Lighten or darken the photo before it is separated — shifts every tone up or down, moving detail between the light and dark separations.

inputs.label: Quality
inputs.help: Tracing resolution — higher samples the photo at a finer grid and captures more detail. Above 100 pushes into very fine, slower traces.

inputs.label: Smoothing
inputs.help: How much the traced outlines round into curves. Low keeps edges faithful and crisp; high flows them into smooth Bézier curves.

inputs.label: Invert tones
inputs.help: Trace from inverted tones — the photo's bright areas become the foreground separations (a tonal negative). Useful for a light subject on a dark background.

inputs.label: Separation colours
inputs.help: One swatch per tone, darkest first and the lightest (paper) last. Auto-sampled from your photo — edit any swatch to recolour that separation.

inputs.label: Re-sample colours from photo
inputs.help: Discard manual edits and re-derive every separation colour from the current photo.

inputs.label: Transparent background
inputs.help: Drop the lightest 'paper' separation so the background is transparent (PNG / SVG).

inputs.label: Contrast
inputs.help: Push the tones apart (higher) or flatten them together (lower) before separating. More contrast gives bolder, more graphic separations.

inputs.label: Hue
inputs.help: Rotate every colour around the wheel.

inputs.label: Saturation
inputs.help: 0 = greyscale · 100 = original · 200 = vivid.

inputs.label: Lightness
inputs.help: Lift toward white or sink toward black.

inputs.label: Colour treatment
inputs.help: Blend a colour over the result. Leave empty for none.

inputs.label: Blend mode
inputs.help: How the treatment colour mixes with the image.

inputs.label: Intensity
inputs.help: Opacity of the colour overlay.

inputs.label: No filter (show raw)
inputs.help: Show the original photo or live camera feed untouched — handy for placing just a logo / lower-third over the raw footage.

inputs.label: SUSE logo overlay
inputs.help: Overlay the SUSE logo on the result. Off by default.

inputs.label: Logo colour
inputs.help: White and green read on dark or busy footage; black suits light backgrounds.

inputs.label: Logo size
inputs.help: Scale the logo up or down.

inputs.label: Lower-third name card
inputs.help: A gently-animated name card that draws in over the footage. Uses your profile name & headshot.

inputs.label: Role / subtitle
inputs.help: Optional line below the name — e.g. a role or event.

inputs.label: Name weight
inputs.help: Font weight of the name line (100 Thin → 900 Black).

inputs.label: Subtitle weight
inputs.help: Font weight of the role / subtitle line (100 Thin → 900 Black).

inputs.label: Headshot (optional)
inputs.help: Defaults to your profile headshot. Pick or upload another to override.
